caption: Close-up of an open Nile crocodile mouth showing the conical teeth rooted in a distinct, thick gumline with visible vascularized tissue.
Internal view of a Komodo dragon mouth highlighting the pale palate, forked tongue, and serrated teeth partially covered by gingival tissue.
Macro view of the soft tissue lining and glottis inside a monitor lizard's mouth, providing a reference for theropod throat anatomy.
Extreme close-up of a cassowary foot showing the three-toed digitigrade structure and the enlarged, dagger-like inner claw.
Detailed view of large scutate scales on an ostrich foot and the heavy, blunt claw curvature adapted for high-impact locomotion.
Lateral view of a Secretary bird foot showing the recurved talon geometry and the robust digital pads used for striking prey.
Macro photography of lizard skin showing the non-overlapping mosaic polygon pattern of scales typical of many large reptiles.
Top-down view of dorsal scutes showing large, raised dome-like osteoderms surrounded by a field of smaller pebbled scales.
Frontal view of the Sue specimen skull showing the narrow snout and wide rear skull which provided deep, binocular eye socket overlap.
Detail of the forelimb bones including the two-fingered manus and the reduced third metacarpal stub characteristic of Tyrannosaurids.
Full skeleton in lateral view showing the modern scientific horizontal posture with the tail acting as a counterbalance to the heavy head.
Scientific reconstruction by Nobu Tamura showing a horizontal spine, accurate muscle mass, and updated integument theories.
Fossilized skin impression showing a fine, non-overlapping mosaic of basement scales, suggesting a scaly hide on parts of the body.
